Erodium malacoides, also known as the Corn Marigold or Corn Buttercup, is a flowering plant that belongs to the geranium family (Geraniaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The bioactive constituents of Erodium malacoides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Erodium malacoides is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion to help alleviate respiratory issues and soothe digestive problems.
